subject: Product Management
title: Survivor Bias in Data
description: A short lesson to help you apply Survivor Bias in Data.

hook.text: In World War II, analysts studied bullet holes on returning planes to decide where to add armor.

buildup.text: The bullet holes showed where planes could take damage and still return. The missing data was the planes that didn't come back.

discovery.text: Wald said: armor the areas with no bullet holes — those are the spots where damage was fatal.

twist.text: Product teams make the same mistake. They study active users and optimize for them — ignoring the users who left.

climax.text: Survivor bias means your data only represents people who stuck around. The most important insights come from those who didn't.

punchline.text: Don't just study your survivors. Study who you lost and why.

quiz.question: What is survivorship bias in product metrics?
options: Data only reflects users who stayed — missing insights from those who left
Surviving users always have the most accurate feedback
Only long-term users should be surveyed
